---
title: "SampleRcode_labmeeting"
author: "Aizah Khurram"
date: "2026-03-02"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Load libraries
```{r}
#have to install libraries if not already installed:
#install.packages(c("tidyverse","broom","dplyr", multcomp","multcompView"))
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)
library(dplyr)
#Tukey tests + letter displays:
library(multcomp)
library(multcompView)

```
Load data:
```{r}
# Change this path to wherever your file is stored
df <- read_delim("/Users/aizahkhurram/Downloads/SampleRCode/SampleRCode/sample_df.txt", delim = "\t")
```

Standard error:
```{r}
# Standard error = SD / sqrt(n)
se <- function(x, na.rm = TRUE){
  x <- if(na.rm) x[!is.na(x)] else x #drop NAs if removing them
  sd(x) / sqrt(length(x))
}
```

Convert pairwise p-values to letters
```{r}
letters_from_pairwise <- function(pmat, alpha = 0.05){
rn <- rownames(pmat)
cn <- colnames(pmat)

pvec <- c() #creates empty vector to store values
for(i in seq_along(rn)){
  for(j in seq_along(cn)){
     p <- pmat[i,j]
     if(!is.na(p)){ #only keeps real comparisons
        nm <- paste(rn[i], cn[j], sep="-")
        pvec[nm] <- p #store p value
      }
    }
  }

multcompView::multcompLetters(pvec, threshold = alpha)$Letters # this converts the the set of p-values into letters
}
```

Automatic Statistics Choices
if residuals are normal (Shapiro test), and if variances are equal (fligner test), then we use ANOVA + Tukey, if not then we use Kruskal + Wilcoxon
```{r}
#dat= data table
#response = column name as string for numeric response
#group = column name as string for treatment

auto_stats_letters <- function(dat, response, group, alpha = 0.05){

#check to see if you've misspelled something etc
  if (!response %in% names(dat)) stop("response column not found: ", response)
  if (!group    %in% names(dat)) stop("group column not found: ", group)

# keep only needed columns
  d <- dat[, c(group, response), drop = FALSE] #drop=false is to keep it as a data frame
  d <- d[!is.na(d[[group]]) & !is.na(d[[response]]), , drop = FALSE]
  d[[group]] <- as.factor(d[[group]]) #d[[group]] uses string is group to access the column and keeps it as a factor

# If fewer than 2 groups, return all "a" since you can't run comparisons
  if (nlevels(d[[group]]) < 2) {
    letters <- setNames(rep("a", nlevels(d[[group]])), levels(d[[group]]))
    return(list(method = NA, normality_p = NA, homogeneity_p = NA, letters = letters))
  }

 # Fit ANOVA model
  fit <- aov(stats::as.formula(paste0(response, " ~ ", group)), data = d)

 # Assumption checks
  #residuals(fit) = model residuals
  #tryCatch = prevents errors if Shapiro can't run due to too few points
  #Fligner= checks if variance is equal across groups (similar to Bartlett/Levene)
  sh_p <- tryCatch(shapiro.test(residuals(fit))$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_)
  fl_p <- tryCatch(
    fligner.test(stats::as.formula(paste0(response, " ~ ", group)), data = d)$p.value,
    error = function(e) NA_real_
  )
# uses parametric if Shapiro and Fligner are =/> than 0.05 
use_parametric <- is.finite(sh_p) && is.finite(fl_p) && sh_p >= alpha && fl_p >= alpha

if (use_parametric) {
    tuk <- TukeyHSD(fit) # runs Tukey pairwise comparisons after ANOVA
    cl  <- multcompView::multcompLetters4(fit, tuk) #turns Tukey p values into letters
    # cl is a list; extract letters for our grouping variable:
    letters <- cl[[group]]$Letters #basically stores what is happening as a label
    method <- "ANOVA + TukeyHSD"
  } else {
    #pairwise.wilxox.test does pairwise rank-based comparisons
    pw <- pairwise.wilcox.test(
      x = d[[response]],
      g = d[[group]],
      p.adjust.method = "BH",
      exact = FALSE
    )
    letters <- letters_from_pairwise(pw$p.value, alpha = alpha) #converts to p-values
    method  <- "Kruskal-Wallis + pairwise Wilcoxon (BH)" #stores method name
  }
  list(method = method, normality_p = sh_p, homogeneity_p = fl_p, letters = letters)
}

```

Clean data:
```{r}
df_plot <- df %>%
# Rename fungus code → italic label
mutate(
  Fungus = recode(fungus,
                    "Sb" = "italic('S. bescii')")
  ) %>%

mutate(
  N_level_mg_L = as.character(N_level_mg_L),
  treatment    = as.factor(treatment)
  )
```

Barplot with points and letters:
```{r}
response_var <- "diameter_mm_251120"   # what we measure
group_var    <- "N_level_mg_L"         # grouping
```

```{r}
sum_bar <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(.data[[group_var]]) %>% #group data by whichever column name is stored in group_var
  summarise(
    mean = mean(.data[[response_var]], na.rm = TRUE), #mean diameter in each group
    se   = se(.data[[response_var]]), #SE within each group
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  rename(group = .data[[group_var]]) #renames grouping column to group since we can then say x=group
# Run stats + letters
stats_out <- auto_stats_letters(
  dat = df_plot,
  response = response_var,
  group = group_var,
  alpha = 0.05
)

# Convert letters into a dataframe
letters_df <- tibble(
  group  = names(stats_out$letters), #group labels
  letter = unname(stats_out$letters) #letters
)

# Join letters onto sum_bar so geom_text can find `letter`
sum_bar <- sum_bar %>%
  left_join(letters_df, by = "group") %>% #adds letter column to sum_bar by matching group names
  mutate(y_lab = mean + se + 1)  # where to place letters
print(sum_bar)
```


```{r}
ggplot() +
  theme_classic() +

#Bars
geom_col(
  data=sum_bar,
  aes(x=group, y=mean, fill=group),
  color="black",
  width=0.8
) +

#Error bars
geom_errorbar(
  data=sum_bar,
  aes(x=group, ymin=mean-se, ymax=mean+se),    width=0.15
 ) +

#Individual points 
geom_point(
 data=df_plot,  
 aes(x=.data[[group_var]],
     y=.data[[response_var]]),
 position=position_jitter(width=0.1), size=2, alpha=0.6,
 color = "red" #if you want the points to match the bars: color = group or color = .data[[group_var]].
) +

#Letters
 geom_text(
  data=sum_bar,
  aes(x=group, y=y_lab, label=letter),
  size=5
 ) +

labs(
  title="Day 8",
  x="Treatment", y="Colony diameter (mm)"
) +
scale_fill_manual(
  values = c("white", "gray", "black"),
  name = "Treatment"
) +
 theme(text=element_text(size=14))
```
Summary of which methods were used and p-values from assumption tests
```{r}
cat("\nStats used:", stats_out$method,
    "\nShapiro p =", stats_out$normality_p,
    "\nFligner p =", stats_out$homogeneity_p,"\n")
```


```{r}
df_time <- df_plot %>%
  # add the initial timepoint (Day 0) #column mutate(diameter_mm_251112 = 6) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(diameter_mm_251114,
             diameter_mm_251117,
             diameter_mm_251120),
    names_to = "Day",
    values_to = "Diameter"
  ) %>%
  # rename columns to actual day numbers, then make numeric for a real time axis
  mutate(
    Day = recode(Day,
      "diameter_mm_251114" = "2",
      "diameter_mm_251117" = "5",
      "diameter_mm_251120" = "8"
    ),
    Day = as.numeric(Day),
    N_level_mg_L = as.factor(N_level_mg_L)  # for grouping/legend
  )

ggplot(df_time, aes(x = Day, y = Diameter, color = N_level_mg_L, group = N_level_mg_L)) +
  theme_classic() +

  # mean line per N level
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1) +

  # mean points per N level
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3) +

  # SE error bars around the mean
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_se, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, color = "black") +

  # individual raw data points
  geom_point(position = position_jitter(width = 0.15, height = 0), alpha = 0.35, size = 2) +

  #if you have multipe species can add this to make separate panels for each species. Italics_parsed goes back to when we set it as italic('S.bescii') so it will print it as italics
  facet_wrap(~Fungus, labeller = label_parsed) +

  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(2, 5, 8)) +

  labs(
    x = "Time (days)",
    y = "Colony diameter (mm)",
    color = "N level" #)
  #+scale_color_manual(
  #values = c("white", "gray", "black"),
 # name = "N level"
  )

```
```{r}
#ggsave("/Users/aizahkhurram/Documents/name.png", plotname, width = 7, height = 5.5, dpi = 300)
```

```{r}
#y = expression("Total " * {}^{13} * "C in BGB (mg)")
# y = expression(delta^{13}*C)
```
